emails.body:
War on the Rocks Daily Digest — 2 October 2026

Top Reads
1. "The Indo-Pacific Logistics Gap Nobody Talks About" — Ryan Evans interviews the new INDOPACOM J4 on sealift shortfalls and the "last tactical mile" problem.
2. "AI in Targeting: Promise vs. Policy" — Margarita Konaev breaks down the DoD's new directive on autonomous weapon systems and what it means for human-on-the-loop requirements.
3. "Recruiting Crisis? The Data Says Otherwise" — A contrarian take from the CNA crew showing FY26 accession quality metrics holding steady across services.

Analysis
• China's new amphibious exercise cycle suggests accelerated timeline for joint island-landing proficiency.
• NATO's new rotational force model in the Baltics gets its first stress test next month.
• Army's M10 Booker decision: what the directed requirement tells us about light armor doctrine.

Podcast Drop
New "Net Assessment" episode: Chris, Melanie, and Zack debate whether the defense industrial base can surge fast enough for a two-war scenario.

---
War on the Rocks | 1775 Eye St NW, Washington, DC 20006
Unsubscribe | Manage Preferences

War on the Rocks Daily Digest — 3 October 2026

Top Reads
1. "Senate NDAA Markup: The Sleepers You Missed" — Our Hill team flags three provisions buried in the SASC version that could reshape acquisition authorities.
2. "Ukraine's Electronic Warfare Evolution, Month 32" — Justin Bronk on how the cat-and-mouse cycle has shifted since the spring counteroffensive.
3. "The Space Force's First Real Budget Fight" — Todd Harrison walks through the FY27 request and where the service is pushing back on OSD cuts.

Analysis
• Houthi missile inventory depletion rates: new intel community assessment suggests faster burn than public estimates.
• Japan's new security clearance law takes effect — early read on industry adoption.
• Marine Corps Force Design 2030 adjustment: the littoral regiment concept gets a quiet course correction.

Weekend Long Read
"The General Who Built the Pentagon's AI Brain" — Profile of Lt. Gen. (ret) Jack Shanahan and the legacy of the Algorithmic Warfare Cross-Functional Team.

---
